title: BIM Coordinator
description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Arch’lab Pte Ltd is an architectural firm focused on building and interior design for healthcare, science & technology, and special facilities for innovative industry. We are building a strong and aspired BIM team to complement our architects and designers in the design delivery process.

Job Summary

You will contribute to the delivery of high-quality BIM models and drawings for healthcare and laboratory projects, leveraging your expertise in architectural, structural, and MEP disciplines to support design and construction compliance.

Responsibilities

  • Develop and complete BIM models and RFP drawings accurately and within project timelines to support design delivery
  • Interpret architectural and structural construction documents to ensure model accuracy and compliance
  • Utilize Autodesk Revit Architecture, Structure, and MEP to create detailed BIM models aligned with project requirements
  • Perform clash detection using Navisworks to identify and resolve design conflicts proactively
  • Conduct 4D simulations and quantity take-offs to support project planning and cost estimation
  • Manage the Common Data Environment effectively to maintain project data integrity and accessibility
  • Apply knowledge of Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) to enhance BIM collaboration and workflow efficiency
  • Prepare drawings and models compliant with CP 83 and BIM e-submission Code of Practice standards
  • Ensure adherence to Fire Code, Approved Document, Singapore Standard, and Authority Code of Practice in all BIM deliverables
  • Support technical and compliance personnel functions as a QP Assistant to uphold project quality and regulatory standards
  • Apply knowledge of Corenet 2.0 and Corenet X frameworks to align BIM processes with sustainability and operational goals
  • Manage time efficiently to meet project deadlines and maintain high-quality outputs

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Experience working on hospital and laboratory projects to address specialized design and compliance requirements
  • Degree in Architecture, Engineering, or Construction, or Specialist Diploma in BIM or equivalent
